**Constitution and By-Laws**

**Alaska Association of Disability Examiners**

**Natural Association of Disability Examiners**

**Constitution**

**Article One**

**Name**

**The name of the organization will be the Alaska Association of Disability Examiners.**

**Article Two**

**Affiliation**

**The Alaska Association of Disability Examiners will be a Chapter of the National Association of Disability Examiners. The Alaska Association of Disability Examiners will comply with the Constitution and By-Laws of the National Association of Disability Examiners.**

**Article Three**

**Purpose**

**The purpose of the Alaska Association of Disability Examiners will be to advance the adjudicative process in disability determinations by any on all means which are readily available to it. Subsidiary purposes shall be:**

1. **to assume full protection of the nights of applicants for disability insurance benefits and to provide a full measure of service to them.**
2. **to promote a sense of understanding and purpose of the disability determination process.**
3. **to make contributions which will assist in setting policy and broadening the scope of the program.**
4. **to encourage the improvement quality of work of all individuals enjoyed in disability evaluation.**
5. **to maintain an awareness of local, regional, and national events affecting the disability determinations process.**
6. **to serve as spokesman for disability examiners in matters of concern to the group.**

**Article Four**

**Territory**

**The geographical boundaries of the Chapter shall be coincident with those of the State of Alaska.**

**Article Five**

**Membership**

1. **Anyone engaged in activities involving the documentation and/or evaluation of medical and/or vocational information submitted in support of applications for disability benefits is eligible for membership in the Alaska Association of Disability Examiners.**
2. **Membership for the Alaska Association of Disability Examiners shall be consistent with membership requirements for the National Association of Disability Examiners.**
3. **Members of the Alaska Association of Disability Examiners must be members of the National Association of Disability Examiners.**
4. **The membership gear will begin January 1 and December 31.**

**Article Six**

**Officers**

**The officers of the Alaska Association of Disability Examiners will be President, Vice President, Secretary-Treasurer, elected annually from and by the membership.**

**Article Seven**

**Board of Directors**

**There will be a Board of Directors whose composition and duties shall be defined in the By-Laws.**

**Article Eight**

**Amendments**

1. **This Constitution may be amended at any regular meeting of the Alaska Association of Disability Examiners at which a quorum is present.**
2. **Members of the Alaska Association of Disability Examiners must be notified in advance of any meeting at which a proposal amendment to the Constitution is to be considered.**
3. **Any proposal amendment must be submitted to the Board of Directors.**
4. **The Board of Directors must submit this proposed amendment to the membership of the Alaska Association of Disability Examiners.**
5. **The Board of Directors must submit a recommendation for or against the proposal amendments to the Constitution.**
6. **A simple majority (51%) vote of the members present will be required for passage of a proposed amendment to the Constitution.**

**Article Nine**

**By-Laws**

1. **The Alaska Association of Disability Examiners shall adopt By-Laws necessary for the operation of the organization.**
2. **By-Laws of the Alaska Association of Disability Examiners will be consistent with those of the National Association of Disability Examiners.**

**By-Laws**

**Article One**

**Meetings**

**Section 1: The Alaska Association of Disability Examiners will meet at the call of the President.**

**Section 2: There will be one or more business meetings annually of the membership.**

**Section 3: There will be other meetings as the President and the Board of Directors deem necessary.**

**Section 4: Fifty per cent of the membership plus one shall constitute a quorum.**

**Section 5: Members will be notified in writing in advance of the purpose of meetings called for amendment to the Constitution or election of officers.**

**Article Two**

**Officers**

**Section 1: Officers will be President, Vice President, Secretary-Treasurer.**

**Section 2: Officers will be elected by a show of hands vote prior to the end of the membership year.**

**Section 3: Officers will take office on the First of January.**

**Section 4: Officers will serve for one year.**

**Section 5: Officers will be eligible for a second term.**

**Section 6: Duties of elected officers will be those usually pertaining to such positions.**

**Article Three**

**Board of Directors**

**Section 1: Duties of the Board will be:**

1. **To serve as Executive Board for the Alaska Association of Disability Examiners.**
2. **To transact business of the Alaska Association of Disability Examiners between Chapter meetings.**
3. **To maintain effective division with the Pacific Region of the National Association of Disability Examiners and the Board of Directors of the National Association of Disability Examiners.**

**Section 2: Membership of the Board of Directors shall consist of:**

1. **The President, Vice President, and Secretary-Treasurer of the Alaska Association of Disability Examiners who shall act in these capacities on the Board.**
2. **The immediate past President of the Alaska Association of Disability Examiners will also be a member.**

**Section 3: When a vacancy on the Board of Directors occurs, the membership at large will elect a succession to fill the unexpected team.**

**Article Four**

**Amendments**

**These BY-Laws may be amended by a simple majority (51%) vote of the members present at any business meeting providing proposed amendments are first submitted to the Board of Directors who shall transmit some to the Alaska Association of Disability Examiners meeting with recommendation for or against passage.**

**Article Five**

**Dues**

**There will be no Chapter dues. Dues will is restricted to annual national dues.**

**Article Six**

**Nominations and Elections**

**Section 1: The yearly office elections will be by a show of hands vote.**

**Section 2: Officers will be elected at the last regularly scheduled meeting which falls on or before 12/1. Nominations will be made from the floor. Additionally, nominations may be in advance of the scheduler meeting by submitting the name(s) in writing to any member of the Board of Directors.**

**Section 3: In order to run for office, a person must be a member of the Alaska Association of Disability Examiners at the time of nomination.**

**Section 4: Officers will be elected by a majority of votes cast. If no one person (for each) wins by a clear majority, then there will be a run off election between the two persons receiving the most votes. The run off will be done by secret ballot.**
